summary: trust panel: When clicking on privacy settings the trust panel stays open

attachments.summary: Bug 2006633 - Ensure trustpanel is closed when selecting privacy button. r=daisuke
attachments.creator: dharvey@mozilla.com
attachments.description: Bug 2006633 - Ensure trustpanel is closed when selecting privacy button. r=daisuke

comments.author: manuel@mozilla.com
comments.raw_text:
STR:

1. open trust panel while on a website (e.g. here bugzilla.mozilla.org) 
2. click on "Privacy Settings"

Expected result: Trust panel closes
Got result: Trust panel stays open showing the insecure warning for `about:blank`
comments.creator: manuel@mozilla.com
comments.text:
Created attachment 9533661
Bildschirmfoto vom 2025-12-17 15-27-10.png

STR:

1. open trust panel while on a website (e.g. here bugzilla.mozilla.org) 
2. click on "Privacy Settings"

Expected result: Trust panel closes
Got result: Trust panel stays open showing the insecure warning for `about:blank`

comments.author: mbeier@mozilla.com
comments.text: We tried reproducing this on Ubuntu but the panel stayed open. Could you provide more info on your OS and window manager and try to reproduce this on other devices?
comments.raw_text: We tried reproducing this on Ubuntu but the panel stayed open. Could you provide more info on your OS and window manager and try to reproduce this on other devices?
comments.creator: mbeier@mozilla.com

comments.raw_text:
The bug is that the panel stays open. Normally on `about:preferences` you can't open the trust panel. It is also in an inconsistent state: It displays the "Connection not secure" (similar to Bug 1997064, but within the panel).

I'm on Arch linux, Gnome 49, Wayland.

I indeed currently doesn't reproduce for me on my normal browser. However it does with a new profile (e.g. launching with `./mach mozregression --launch 2026-01-04`).
comments.creator: manuel@mozilla.com
comments.text:
The bug is that the panel stays open. Normally on `about:preferences` you can't open the trust panel. It is also in an inconsistent state: It displays the "Connection not secure" (similar to Bug 1997064, but within the panel).

I'm on Arch linux, Gnome 49, Wayland.

I indeed currently doesn't reproduce for me on my normal browser. However it does with a new profile (e.g. launching with `./mach mozregression --launch 2026-01-04`).
comments.author: manuel@mozilla.com
dharvey@mozilla.com
comments.creator: dharvey@mozilla.com
comments.raw_text:
Is there any possibility you have leave window open from devtools set? possibly set while debugging?

Alternatively with trustpanel disabled, can you reproduce this with the shield icon -> report broken site -> clicking one of the links there? Be interesting to know if this was trustpanel specific. Thanks
comments.text:
Is there any possibility you have leave window open from devtools set? possibly set while debugging?

Alternatively with trustpanel disabled, can you reproduce this with the shield icon -> report broken site -> clicking one of the links there? Be interesting to know if this was trustpanel specific. Thanks

I haven't set anything else. I'm using a fresh profile with mozregression: `./mach mozregression --launch 2026-01-04`.

When disabling the trust panel. Clicking on "Protection Dashboard" closes the shield menu and opens a new tab in the legacy shield menu. Same for "Report broken site" and "Protection dashboard".

Reenabling the trustPanel icon and trying clicking on on "Privacy Settings" still has the behavior observed in comment 0.

After trying around a bit more: It might only be observable the first time the trustPanel opens (or other conditions), because I can't reproduce when opening and closing the protection panel before starting the STR from comment 0.
comments.raw_text:
I haven't set anything else. I'm using a fresh profile with mozregression: `./mach mozregression --launch 2026-01-04`.

When disabling the trust panel. Clicking on "Protection Dashboard" closes the shield menu and opens a new tab in the legacy shield menu. Same for "Report broken site" and "Protection dashboard".

Reenabling the trustPanel icon and trying clicking on on "Privacy Settings" still has the behavior observed in comment 0.

After trying around a bit more: It might only be observable the first time the trustPanel opens (or other conditions), because I can't reproduce when opening and closing the protection panel before starting the STR from comment 0.
comments.creator: manuel@mozilla.com
comments.author: manuel@mozilla.com
